rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RENNA HOLMES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 11, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT TOSHIA WEBSTER, TINA WEBSTER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off Calendar, matter to be reset in the Probate Department. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EICON INC VS WORLDPOINT INTERACTIVE INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cross-Complainants' Second Cause Of Action (Breach Of Express Indemnity) And Declaratory Relief Related Thereto; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Cross-Deft Massimo Fuch'S
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 11, 2010, line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT MASSIMO FUCHS Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as to Cross-Complainants' Second Cause of Action (Breach of Express Indemnity) and Declaratory Relief Related Thereto. Motion denied. Motion does not violate CCP section 1008. Cross-complaint sufficiently pleads alter ego liability and bad faith. Snyder is seeking to recover for harm done to it and has standing. Doctrine of res judicata not applicable. Bankruptcy settlement does not show that Snyder released claims against Worldpoint's officers. Ninth Circuit memorandum disposition indicates the claims in favor of or against Fuchs were not implicated. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VERA POON VS. STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 11, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied, failure to shift burden of proof. The court focuses on the actions of the insured rather than the form of the proceedings. Once defendant learned of the concurrent construction defect action, it owed a duty to defend and indemnify both related actions. Defendant also owed a duty to appoint independent counsel. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VERA POON VS. STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 11, 2010, line 10, PLAINTIFF VERA POON MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication is granted in part. Motion granted as to #1, defendant has a duty to indemnify plaintiff under the policies for the arbitration award. #3, defendant has a duty to indemnify plaintiff for the fees/costs incurred in the arbitration award. #6, the 16th affirmative defense (coverage barred by Ins. Code section 553- can't insure for intentional bad acts) fails based on waiver and estoppel. #7, the 10th defense fails based on waiver and estoppel. #8, the 9th defense fails based on waiver and estoppel. And #9, the 7th defense fails based on waiver and estoppel. Summary adjudication denied as to remaining issues under CCP section 437c(f)(1).

rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN PALM SOZA VS. TAQUERIA LA CUMBRE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion In Support Of X-Complainant'S Leave To File A First Amended X-Complaint

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ESMERALDA CONTRERAS VS. CAROL BUTERWORTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 11, 2010, line 16, DEFENDANTS CAROL BUTTERWORTH, GORDON BUTTERWORTH Motion For Attorneys Fees. Motion for attorney fees granted in part. Defendant prevailed on the anti-SLAPP motion in that they were successful in striking the wrongful eviction cause of action that narrows the scope of the litigation and reduces possible damages. The court adopts defendants' lodestar amount of attorney fees equal to $11,783.50, however, the court finds motion was only partially successful and that two causes of action survied the motion. The work on the successful and unsuccessful causes of action overlap. The court reduces the requested amount to one-third which is the relative success in achieving the motion's objectives, and awards attorney fees in the amount of $3,927.83. =(302/CWW)
